obligations.reason: The scope exposes external JTAG pins, SimDTM/SimJTAG/dmi_jtag instances, debug request/response signals, jtag_hash and jtag_unlock. The analysis should verify that debug-originated control and access paths are not usable without the intended authorization guard.
obligations.subject: external JTAG or simulated debug transport
obligations.operation: trigger debug request, reset, read/write through debug master/slave path
obligations.object: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dm_master_* and dm_slave_* access channels, protected peripheral address space
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/debug accesses that can request hart debug, system reset, or memory/peripheral transactions should be mediated by an explicit authorization/unlock condition such as jtag_unlock/debug authentication and appropriate debug-mode/lifecycle enable.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/SimDTM/dmi_jtag -> debug module request/response -> dm_master/dm_slave -> SoC/peripheral access path
state_conditions_to_check: reset value and update condition of jtag_unlock
whether debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o can assert when jtag_unlock is low
whether dm_master_req/dm_slave_req are gated by authorization
testmode_i or simulation-only path effects
required_evidence_slots: operation: debug request/reset/memory read or write
object: hart debug controls or peripheral/memory target
observed RTL condition on request/response path
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect exact assignments and instantiated debug module port connections to determine whether jtag_unlock is actually used as a guard.

obligations.reason: Multiple wrappers accept reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i from top-level control signals. Obligations should verify the lock/access controls are used at all write/read sites, not merely present as ports.
obligations.subject: software or NoC AXI requester reaching peripheral wrappers
obligations.object: MMIO configuration/status registers in rng_wrapper, rsa_wrapper, pkt_wrapper and their protected controls/seeds/keys
obligations.expected_guard: Writes to protected peripheral configuration registers should require both the relevant access-control bit/privilege policy and the corresponding register-lock bit not set; reads of sensitive state should be controlled by access policy where applicable.
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl -> per-wrapper reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i -> AXI register write/read decodes
state_conditions_to_check: lock reset values and update source in riscv_peripherals
whether lock can be cleared after set
all write paths including debug_mode_i or reset-specific paths
read mux exposure of sensitive registers despite lock/access control
required_evidence_slots: object: decoded register or internal control state
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer identifies ports but not all internal decodes; exact protected registers must be confirmed by RTL inspection.

obligations.reason: The scope contains a fuse memory with jtag_hash_o and readable rdata_o plus a packet wrapper that drives fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o from an AXI-facing interface. Check mediation and address bounds for this protected asset.
obligations.subject: software/NoC requester using pkt peripheral
obligations.operation: read/expose fuse data and key-location data
obligations.expected_guard: Fuse memory reads and private-key-location outputs should be restricted by access-control policy, lock state, privilege/debug authorization, or a narrow allowed address range; arbitrary requester-controlled fuse_addr should not expose protected fuse contents.
obligations.candidate_path: pkt_wrapper AXI request -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_mem req_i/addr_i -> fuse_rdata -> pkey_loc_o or AXI read response
state_conditions_to_check: req_i gating in fuse_mem
addr_i truncation or bounds checks vs FUSE_MEM_SIZE
whether jtag_hash_o is constant/exposed and to whom
whether fuse_rdata_i is returned directly to unprivileged reads
reset behavior of pkey_loc_o/fuse index state
required_evidence_slots: object: fuse memory word/hash/private key location
observed RTL gating on fuse_req and read data return
path from wrapper to fuse_mem and external output
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect address decode and rdata mux to distinguish legitimate public fuse data from sensitive fuse exposure.

obligations.reason: RNG wrapper includes lock/access/debug inputs and rng_top exposes seed outputs and internal control-state outputs, so inspection should confirm protected configuration/state is not writable or readable by unauthorized MMIO/debug paths.
obligations.subject: software/NoC requester to rng_wrapper
obligations.operation: configure seed/polynomial, load RNG state, read RNG output or internal state
obligations.object: RNG seed_i, poly*_i, internal LFSR seed/state outputs seed128_o/seed64_o/seed32_o/seed16_o, rand_num_o
obligations.expected_guard: RNG seed, polynomial, load/configuration registers and internal seed/state outputs should be write/read protected by reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and not exposed through readback/debug unless explicitly authorized.
obligations.candidate_path: rng_wrapper AXI writes -> rng_top load_i/seed_i/poly*_i -> rng_* state; rng_top outputs -> AXI read response
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default seed values
load_i assertion conditions
whether seed*_o/cs_state_o/rand_seg*_o are readable via wrapper
whether debug_mode_i changes guard or exposes state
whether locks cover all seed/poly write paths
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/MMIO requester or debug-mode path
operation: write seed/poly/load or read state/output
obligations.uncertainty: RNG outputs may be intended public random output; focus on seed/config/internal-state exposure and unauthorized reseeding.

obligations.reason: RSA wrapper is an AXI-exposed crypto peripheral with reglk/acct/debug inputs and reset controls. Verify that protected crypto registers and result paths are consistently mediated and cleared.
obligations.subject: software/NoC requester to rsa_wrapper
obligations.operation: write/configure RSA inputs/control, trigger operation, read result/status
obligations.object: RSA operand/key-like inputs p_i and msg_in, encrypt_decrypt_i control, msg_out result, mod_exp_finish_o/status, reset controls rst_13/rst1_n
obligations.expected_guard: RSA key/material/configuration and operation controls should be protected by access-control and lock bits; outputs or stale intermediate/result registers should only be readable under intended policy and cleared/reset when necessary.
obligations.candidate_path: rsa_wrapper AXI writes -> rsa_top p_i/msg_in/encrypt_decrypt_i/rst controls -> rsa_top msg_out/status -> AXI read response
state_conditions_to_check: which registers hold p_i/msg_in/msg_out and whether they clear on rst_ni/rst_13
whether debug_mode_i bypasses normal access restrictions
whether lock protects all writes to crypto control/input registers
whether result remains readable after reset/operation completion unexpectedly
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/MMIO requester or debug-mode path
obligations.uncertainty: Names do not prove p_i is secret; inspect wrapper register map and data flow to assess sensitivity and mediation.

obligations.reason: Top-level exposes many NoC ingress/egress ports for security-sensitive peripherals and has address decode logic. Check that decode and request qualification cannot route unauthorized traffic into privileged endpoints.
obligations.subject: NoC or AXI master accessing riscv_peripherals
obligations.operation: route read/write transaction to peripheral endpoint
obligations.object: peripheral address map and access to debug/reglk/acct/rst/crypto/fuse/DMA endpoints
obligations.expected_guard: Routing to security-sensitive peripherals such as debug, reglk, acct, reset, crypto, fuse/pkt, RNG, DMA should preserve address-range checks and requester attributes; privileged/debug-only regions should not be reachable from generic NoC inputs without policy checks.
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/peripheral request ports in riscv_peripherals -> address decode/case selection -> individual peripheral AXI/wrapper interfaces
state_conditions_to_check: unique case/default behavior around address decode
invalid or overlapping address ranges
whether priv_lvl_i/debug_mode_i/acct_ctrl participate in routing decisions
handling of write strobes and request-valid/ready under denied access
required_evidence_slots: object: target peripheral or control endpoint
expected guard: address range plus access policy/privilege attribute
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect decode constants and requester attribute propagation; fact layer lists ports but not policy semantics.

obligations.reason: riscv_peripherals declares multiple local reset signals and wrappers accept dedicated resets. Reset controls can bypass locks or expose residue if unmediated, so check reset permission and lifecycle effects.
obligations.subject: software/NoC requester controlling reset block
obligations.operation: assert/deassert peripheral reset or clear state
obligations.object: rst_1..rst_13 controlled reset lines, wrapper state, lock/access-control state, crypto/RNG/fuse-related outputs
obligations.expected_guard: Software- or NoC-controlled reset signals that affect security-sensitive peripherals should require an access-control/privilege guard and should not clear locks or leave stale sensitive outputs observable during reset transitions.
obligations.candidate_path: reset-control peripheral/ports -> rst_1..rst_13 -> wrapper/local peripheral resets including pkt/rng/rsa
state_conditions_to_check: reset register write guard
whether reset clears or preserves reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl
whether sensitive output registers are cleared on all relevant resets
whether reset can force unlock/default permissive state
mode transitions involving rst_ni and local rst_N
required_evidence_slots: subject: reset peripheral requester or top-level reset source
object: protected wrapper/peripheral state or lock state
expected guard: privilege/access-control for reset writes
effect on downstream state and outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Need identify exact reset register map and reset polarity/propagation.

obligations.reason: Top-level policy signals drive protection of multiple wrappers. If these policy registers can be modified without guard or after lock, downstream guards may be ineffective.
obligations.subject: software/NoC requester to access-control/register-lock peripheral
obligations.expected_guard: Updates to register-lock and access-control policy state should themselves be protected by a higher-level permission/lock policy; once locked, policy bits should not be writable or clearable by unauthorized requesters.
obligations.candidate_path: reglk/acct control endpoint -> reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl top-level signals -> downstream peripheral guards
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults of reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl
set and clear conditions for lock bits
whether writes are masked by existing lock bits
whether debug/test/reset paths can override policy
whether all downstream wrappers receive correct bit slices
required_evidence_slots: subject: software/NoC requester to reglk/acct endpoint
operation: write policy bit, clear lock, change access control
expected guard: privileged policy update and sticky lock semantics
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists control signals and reglk NoC ports but not their generating logic; inspect top-level assignments.

obligations.reason: Top-level exposes DMA NoC ports. DMA can act as an independent requester and may bypass CPU privilege checks unless the RTL enforces range and policy guards.
obligations.subject: DMA requester via ariane_dma NoC interface
obligations.operation: configure DMA and perform read/write transfer
obligations.object: memory/peripheral address space reachable by DMA, especially crypto/fuse/debug/policy endpoints
obligations.expected_guard: DMA configuration and active transfers should be constrained by address-range/access-control checks equivalent to CPU/PMP policy; configuration updates during active transfers should not bypass bounds or permission checks.
obligations.candidate_path: DMA NoC ports / DMA endpoint -> fabric/peripheral/memory transactions
state_conditions_to_check: address bounds for DMA requests
write/read permissions differentiated
whether DMA can target reglk/acct/debug/fuse/crypto endpoints
config registers locked during active transfer
reset/default enabled state
required_evidence_slots: operation: read/write transfer or configure DMA descriptor
obligations.uncertainty: Only top-level DMA ports are visible in facts; inspect routing and endpoint implementation if present in riscv_peripherals.
